metadata.description: Name of the environment (used for resource naming)
Primary location for all resources - Top 5 North America regions with best free tier capacity
Optional: Specific lesson number to deploy. Leave empty for full deployment.
App Service Plan SKU. Use B1 if your subscription has no F1 quota.
Admin password for Lesson 05 Windows VM. Must be 12+ chars with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special char.
SSH public key for Lesson 06 Linux VM. Generate with: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096
Tags to apply to all resources
Azure region for the storage account
Address space for the virtual network
Address prefix for the web subnet
Address prefix for the app subnet
Address prefix for the data subnet
Name of the App Service Plan
Admin username for the Windows VM
Admin password for the Windows VM
Name of the Azure Container Registry
Name of the storage account for the function
Name of the Cosmos DB account
Use serverless capacity mode (recommended for learning - pay only for what you use)

properties.description: AI Hub for Azure Essentials training course
AI Project for chatbot development exercises

metadata.description: VM size - B2s is good balance of cost/performance for learning

variables.cloudInitScript:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
package_upgrade: true

packages:
  - snapd
  - curl
  - wget
  - git
  - jq

runcmd:
  # Install MicroK8s
  - snap install microk8s --classic --channel=1.28/stable

  # Add azureuser to microk8s group
  - usermod -a -G microk8s azureuser
  - chown -R azureuser:azureuser /home/azureuser/.kube || mkdir -p /home/azureuser/.kube && chown -R azureuser:azureuser /home/azureuser/.kube

  # Wait for MicroK8s to be ready
  - microk8s status --wait-ready

  # Enable essential addons
  - microk8s enable dns
  - microk8s enable dashboard
  - microk8s enable storage
  - microk8s enable registry

  # Create kubectl alias for convenience
  - echo "alias kubectl='microk8s kubectl'" >> /home/azureuser/.bashrc
  - echo "alias k='microk8s kubectl'" >> /home/azureuser/.bashrc

  # Create a welcome message
  - |
    cat > /etc/motd << 'EOF'

    ██████  ██████  ██████  ███████     ████████  ██████       ██████ ██       ██████  ██    ██ ██████
    ██      ██    ██ ██   ██ ██             ██    ██    ██     ██      ██      ██    ██ ██    ██ ██   ██
    ██      ██    ██ ██   ██ █████          ██    ██    ██     ██      ██      ██    ██ ██    ██ ██   ██
    ██      ██    ██ ██   ██ ██             ██    ██    ██     ██      ██      ██    ██ ██    ██ ██   ██
     ██████  ██████  ██████  ███████        ██     ██████       ██████ ███████  ██████   ██████  ██████

    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
      Azure Essentials - Lesson 06: Linux & Kubernetes
      MicroK8s is installed and ready!
    ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    Quick Commands:
      microk8s status          - Check MicroK8s status
      microk8s kubectl get all - List all resources
      kubectl get nodes        - List cluster nodes (alias configured)
      k get pods -A            - List all pods (short alias)

    Dashboard:
      microk8s dashboard-proxy

    EOF

final_message: "MicroK8s installation complete! Ready for Kubernetes learning."

STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME.value: [coalesce(tryGet(tryGet(tryGet(if(variables('deployStorage'), reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, format('rg-{0}-lesson03-storage', parameters('environmentName'))), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', format('storage-{0}', variables('resourceToken'))), '2025-04-01'), null()), 'outputs'), 'storageAccountName'), 'value'), '')]
